input_contract.format: Line 1: x q. Next q lines: type val.

type.desc: 1 for team arrival, 2 for coverage query

val.desc: a_i (preference time) for type 1, K (target interval size) for type 2

input_contract.semantics: Operations are processed strictly in input order. Team IDs are assigned sequentially starting from 1 based on the arrival order of type 1 operations.

YES_branch.format: YES\n followed by K lines of 'id s'
validity_requirements: id must correspond to a team that has arrived prior to this query
s % x == a_id % x OR s % x == (a_id + 1) % x
All K ids in the output for this query must be distinct
All K s values must be distinct and collectively form the set {0, 1, ..., K-1}
Count of arrived teams whose valid slot set intersects [L, R] must be strictly less than R - L + 1
(L, R) must be lexicographically minimal among all intervals satisfying the violation condition

oracle_limits.brute_force_method: Construct bipartite graph between available teams and slots [0, K-1], run max flow/matching for YES. For NO, enumerate all O(K^2) subintervals, count covering teams, and find lexicographically smallest violating interval.

test_buckets.name: basic_small
test_buckets.purpose: Verify fundamental YES/NO branching, output formatting, and basic modulo constraints

test_buckets.name: boundary_values
test_buckets.purpose: Test edge cases: x=1, K=1, K=x, a_i=0, large a_i, and exact capacity boundaries
test_buckets.scale_intent: x in {1, 2}, K in {1, x}, a_i up to 1e9

test_buckets.name: random_moderate
test_buckets.purpose: Stress test correctness under varied random distributions of a_i and K

test_buckets.name: adversarial_hall
test_buckets.purpose: Force tight Hall violations, test lexicographical minimality extraction, and prevent greedy false positives
test_buckets.scale_intent: Crafted a_i sequences creating narrow coverage gaps, K near x or slightly larger

test_buckets.name: performance_max
test_buckets.purpose: Validate O(log x) or O(1) per operation complexity under full constraints and large K
test_buckets.scale_intent: x, q = 4e5, sum(K for YES) = 4e5, K up to 1e9 for NO queries

sample_tests.purpose: Demonstrate basic YES assignment and NO conflict interval output
Demonstrate lexicographically minimal conflict interval selection

ambiguity_notes: Team consumption across queries is not explicitly stated. Conservative assumption: teams are NOT consumed and remain available for all subsequent queries, but each team can be assigned at most once per individual query.
Output order of the K lines in the YES branch is unspecified. The checker must accept any permutation of valid (id, s) pairs.

Revision context reports standard_oracle_mismatch, checker_rejects_standard_output, and performance_failure. This indicates the current validation logic likely fails to independently verify: (1) exact modulo constraints, (2) bijection for YES, (3) independent Hall violation counting and lexicographical minimality for NO. The checker must be rebuilt to recompute coverage counts and verify minimality from scratch rather than relying on string matching or partial checks.

K can reach 10^9. Oracle and checker must handle large K without iterating over [0, K-1]. Hall violation counting must rely on compressed interval logic or mathematical properties of the modulo-adjacency constraint.
Lexicographical minimality for NO is guaranteed unique by definition, but the checker must explicitly verify that no (L', R') with L' < L or (L' == L and R' < R) satisfies the violation condition.
